zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury於大約1233年出生在英國賀特福爾時，她的父親Walter de Clifford48歲，母親Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire27歲。她於大約1254年與William Longespee of Salisbury III在英國林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury于大约1233年出生在英国賀特福爾时，她的父亲Walter de Clifford48岁，母亲Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire27岁。她于大约1254年与William Longespee of Salisbury III在英国林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾布利普斯費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury was born about 1233, in Hertfordshire, England, her father, Walter de Clifford, was 48 and her mother, Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire, was 27. She married William Longespee of Salisbury III about 1254, in Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She was buried in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury est née environ 1233, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter de Clifford, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé William Longespee of Salisbury III environ 1254, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elleenterrée à Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury um 1233 in Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter de Clifford 48 und ihre Mutter Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire 27. Sie hat um 1254 in Lincolnshire, England William Longespee of Salisbury III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie wurde in Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury è nata il circa 1233 nel Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Walter de Clifford, aveva 48 anni e sua madre, Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato William Longespee of Salisbury III circa 1254, nel Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Fu sepolta a Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Clifford Countess of Salisburyが約1233年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Walter de Cliffordは48歳，母Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshireは27歳であった。約1254年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでWilliam Longespee of Salisbury IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリンプスフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury(이)가 대략 1233년 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter de Clifford(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 대략 1254년 영국 링코실르에서 William Longespee of Salisbury III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 그루스스터실르 브림프스필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury nasceu aproximadamente 1233, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter de Clifford, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com William Longespee of Salisbury III aproximadamente 1254, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela foi sepultada em Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury родилась около 1233 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter de Clifford было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Longespee of Salisbury III около 1254 г. в местности линколншир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она была похоронена в местности бримпсфиелд, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Clifford Countess of Salisbury nació aproximadamente 1233, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter de Clifford, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Margred verch Llywelyn of Caernarfonshire, tenía 27 años. Se casó con William Longespee of Salisbury III aproximadamente 1254, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Fue sepultada en Brimpsfield, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
